# Engineering Plan: Real-Time Notification System

**Generated by:** EM Copilot — BRD to Engineering Plan Agent  
**Source BRD:** Real-Time Notification System v1.2  
**Generated:** 2024-11-16  
**Plan Coverage:** 92% of requirements (see open items below)

---

## ⚠️ Open Items Requiring Decision Before Sprint Planning

The following open questions from the BRD must be resolved before planning can be finalized:

| # | Question | Blocking | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| OQ-1 | Push notification provider decision (Firebase vs. OneSignal vs. Expo) | M3 (Push Integration) | Product + Engineering |
| OQ-2 | Notification retention policy (hard delete vs. archive at 90 days) | M4 (Inbox) | Product |
| OQ-3 | Rate limiting thresholds per user per hour | M1 (Core Delivery) | Product |
| OQ-4 | Digest frequency options for batched preference | M2 (Preferences) | Product |

> **Recommendation:** Schedule a 30-minute decision meeting with Product before sprint 1 begins. M3 cannot be scoped without OQ-1.

---

## Executive Summary

This plan covers the engineering implementation of the Real-Time Notification System as defined in BRD v1.2. The plan is structured in two phases aligned with product's stated Q1/Q2 targets.

**Phase 1 (Q1):** Core in-app notification delivery, preference management, and badge counts — covering FR-001, FR-002, FR-005.

**Phase 2 (Q2):** Push notifications (FR-003) and notification inbox (FR-004).

**Total effort estimate:** ~42 engineering-weeks across backend, frontend, infra, mobile, and QA.

---

## Phase 1 Milestones

### M1 — WebSocket Infrastructure and Real-Time Delivery
**Effort:** XL (12–16 eng-weeks)  
**Domain:** Backend + Infra  
**Dependencies:** None  
**Requirements:** FR-001, NFR-001, NFR-002, NFR-003, NFR-004  

Design and implement the WebSocket connection layer capable of supporting 50,000 concurrent connections with horizontal scaling. This is the highest-risk milestone and should begin immediately.

**Key decisions to make before M1 begins:**
- WebSocket vs. SSE (engineering recommendation: WebSocket for bidirectionality needed for FR-005)
- Load balancer sticky session strategy for WebSocket connections
- Connection authentication approach (JWT validation at handshake)

**Risks:**
- Scale target (50k concurrent) requires load testing infrastructure that may not exist yet
- WebSocket connection management at scale is non-trivial; plan for 2-week buffer
- NFR-003 (no performance regression) requires baseline measurement before work begins

---

### M2 — Notification Preference Service
**Effort:** M (4–6 eng-weeks)  
**Domain:** Backend + Frontend  
**Dependencies:** M1 (can begin schema design in parallel)  
**Requirements:** FR-002  

Build the preference management service including storage, API, and UI. The digest scheduling component is blocked on OQ-4 (digest frequency options).

**Risks:**
- Digest scheduling (if frequencies include hourly) requires a reliable job scheduler — confirm infra support

---

### M3 — Notification Badge Counts (Real-Time)
**Effort:** S (2–3 eng-weeks)  
**Domain:** Frontend + Backend  
**Dependencies:** M1 (requires WebSocket layer)  
**Requirements:** FR-005  

Real-time badge count updates via WebSocket. Low complexity once M1 is complete.

---

## Phase 2 Milestones

### M4 — Push Notification Integration
**Effort:** L (6–8 eng-weeks)  
**Domain:** Backend + Mobile  
**Dependencies:** M1, OQ-1 (provider decision required)  
**Requirements:** FR-003  

Integrate with the chosen push notification provider for web, iOS, and Android. Requires mobile team capacity in Q1 for device token registration, even though delivery launches Q2.

**Risks:**
- Provider decision (OQ-1) is on critical path — delay blocks all M4 scoping
- iOS push requires Apple developer account configuration and review lead times
- Mobile team capacity not confirmed for Q1 token registration work

---

### M5 — Notification Inbox
**Effort:** M (5–7 eng-weeks)  
**Domain:** Backend + Frontend  
**Dependencies:** M1, OQ-2 (retention policy)  
**Requirements:** FR-004  

Inbox UI with read/unread state, bulk actions, and 90-day history. Retention policy (OQ-2) affects storage schema and must be decided before M5 data modeling begins.

---

## Infrastructure Requirements

| Requirement | Current State | Action Required |
|---|---|---|
| WebSocket-capable load balancer | Unknown | Infra team to confirm and provision |
| Horizontal scaling support for WS | Not in place | Design and implement as part of M1 |
| Kafka consumer for notification triggers | Exists (event bus) | Schema definitions needed from data platform |
| Push token storage | None | New storage component as part of M4 |
| Notification history storage | None | New storage component as part of M5 |

---

## Timeline

```
Q1
Week 1-2:   M1 begins (WebSocket design + infra review)
            M2 begins (schema design, preference API spec)
Week 3-8:   M1 implementation (WebSocket layer)
Week 4-8:   M2 implementation (preference service + UI)
Week 7-9:   M3 implementation (badge counts)
Week 9-10:  M1 + M2 + M3 integration testing + load testing
Week 11-12: Buffer + hardening

Q2
Week 1-2:   M4 begins (pending OQ-1 decision)
            M5 begins (pending OQ-2 decision)
Week 3-7:   M4 + M5 implementation
Week 8-9:   Integration testing
Week 10:    Launch
```

---

## Risk Summary

| Risk | Severity | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| WebSocket scale target not met | High | Medium | Load test at 25k by week 6; go/no-go gate |
| Push provider decision delayed | High | Medium | Escalate to product; unblock by week 2 |
| Mobile team capacity unavailable Q1 | Medium | Medium | Confirm with mobile lead; token reg is parallel |
| NFR-003 performance regression | Medium | Low | Baseline measurements before M1; perf tests in CI |
| Kafka schema definitions delayed | Medium | Low | Coordinate with data platform in week 1 |

---

## Plan Review Findings

**Coverage:** 92% of BRD requirements are covered by this plan. FR-003 (push) is partially blocked pending OQ-1.

**Confidence by milestone:**
- M1: High — well-defined scope, known technology
- M2: High — straightforward, pending OQ-4 for digest
- M3: High — low complexity once M1 complete
- M4: Low — blocked on provider decision and mobile capacity
- M5: Medium — blocked on retention policy decision

**Reviewer recommendation:** Approve Phase 1 for sprint planning. Hold Phase 2 planning until open questions are resolved.
